actions.delete-body: Grafana keeps a history of reports, this action will delete all of them. It is not reversible.

app-config.description: Enable or disable the steps for each check type.
app-config.title: Available Check Types

deprecation.description: Check if any installed plugins are deprecated.
deprecation.resolution: Check the documentation for recommended steps or delete the plugin.
deprecation.title: Deprecation check

health-check.description: Checks if a data source is healthy.
health-check.resolution: Go to the data source configuration page and address the issues reported.
health-check.title: Health check

missing-plugin.description: Checks if the plugin associated with the data source is installed and available.
missing-plugin.resolution: Delete the datasource or install the plugin.
missing-plugin.title: Missing plugin check

out_of_support_version.description: Check if the current Grafana version is out of support.
out_of_support_version.resolution: Out of support versions will not receive security updates or bug fixes. Upgrade to a more recent version. Learn more about version support.
out_of_support_version.title: Grafana version check

pinned_version.description: Checks if the Grafana version is pinned.
pinned_version.resolution: You may miss out on security updates and bug fixes if you use a pinned version. Contact your Grafana administrator and open a support ticket to help you get unpinned.
pinned_version.title: Grafana Cloud version check

prom-dep-auth.description: Checks if Prometheus data sources are using deprecated authentication methods (Azure auth and SigV4)
prom-dep-auth.resolution: Make sure that 'Azure Monitor Managed Service for Prometheus' and/or 'Amazon Managed Service for Prometheus' plugins are installed. If the data source is provisioned, edit data source type in the provisioning file to use 'azureprometheus' or 'amazonprometheus'.
prom-dep-auth.title: Prometheus deprecated authentication check

security_config.description: Checks if the Grafana security configuration is set correctly.
security_config.resolution: Follow the documentation for each element.
security_config.title: Security config check

sso-list-format-validation.description: Checks if list configs in SSO settings are in a valid list format (space-separated, comma-separated or JSON array).
sso-list-format-validation.resolution: Configure the relevant SSO setting using a valid format, like space-separated ("opt1 opt2"), comma-separated values ("opt1, opt2") or JSON array format (["opt1", "opt2"]).
sso-list-format-validation.title: SSO List Setting Format Validation

twinmaker_sceneviewer.description: Warns when the Grafana IoT TwinMaker App is installed that the SceneViewer panel will stop working in Grafana 13.1.
twinmaker_sceneviewer.resolution: The SceneViewer panel in the TwinMaker App will stop working in Grafana 13.1. Ignore or silence this warning if you are not using the SceneViewer panel.
twinmaker_sceneviewer.title: TwinMaker SceneViewer deprecation check

uid-validation.description: Checks if the UID of a data source is valid.
uid-validation.resolution: Check the documentation for more information or delete the data source and create a new one.
uid-validation.title: UID validation

unsigned.description: Checks if the plugin's signature is missing or invalid.
unsigned.resolution: For security, we recommend only installing plugins from the catalog. Review the plugin's status and verify your allowlist if appropriate.
unsigned.title: Plugin signature check

update.description: Checks if an installed plugins has a newer version available.
update.resolution: There are newer versions available for the plugins listed below. We recommend going to the plugin admin page and upgrading to the latest version.
update.title: Update check

check-error.message: Check failed to complete. See server logs for details or try again.

check-type-item.heading: Check type: {{name}}
check-type-item.no-ignore-support: Your current version of Grafana does not support ignoring steps

check-warning.message: Check is taking longer than expected (updated {{time}} ago). Inspect server logs for errors or delete and re-create the report to retry.

feature-flag-missing.description: The Grafana Advisor requires the <1>grafanaAdvisor</1> feature toggle to be enabled.
feature-flag-missing.instructions-link: Instructions to enable the feature toggle:
feature-flag-missing.title: Missing feature flag.

home.error-body: Check server logs for more details, refresh the report or open a support ticket if the problem persists.
home.feedback-title: Share your thoughts about Grafana Advisor.
home.partial-results-body: Found too many reports to process. Please delete them and refresh.
home.results-may-change: results may change as checks complete
home.silenced-text: Silenced issues don't appear in this report. Use the eye icon in the top right corner to manage visibility.
home.subtitle: Helps you keep your Grafana instances running smoothly and securely by running checks and suggest actions to fix identified issues.
home.title: Advisor

more-info.description: Below you can find the summary of checks we ran that were passing or not applicable to your instance. Good news, you're doing great in those areas:
more-info.label: No action needed

no-checks.description-line1: Advisor can analyze your Grafana setup and look for potential issues.
no-checks.description-line2: Once a report is generated, it will be automatically updated periodically.
no-checks.title: No checks run yet
